corrections.original: Dockerfile FROM openclaw + npm install claude-code as root, then USER node
corrections.corrected: Dockerfile correctly sets USER node after root operations - COMPLIANT. No correction needed.

corrections.original: Gateway originally bound to 0.0.0.0 (lan) on port 18789
corrections.corrected: Gateway now bound to 127.0.0.1 (loopback) on port 18789. Port 18790 removed. FIXED.
Already correctly mounted with :ro flag. Specific file mount with read-only permission is secure. No correction needed.

corrections.original: Guardian script had Windows CRLF line endings causing bash syntax errors in Linux container
corrections.corrected: Rewritten with proper LF line endings. Add .gitattributes with *.sh text eol=lf to enforce Unix line endings.

corrections.original: Git dubious ownership warning due to volume mount ownership mismatch
corrections.corrected: Fixed with git config --global --add safe.directory. RESOLVED.

corrections.original: Guardian used pgrep to check gateway - fails across Docker container boundaries
corrections.corrected: Changed to HTTP healthcheck: curl http://openclaw-gateway:18789/healthz every 30s. FIXED.
Add set -euo pipefail at top of guardian.sh. Current script may silently continue on curl failure.

corrections.original: No explicit restart policy on critical containers in docker-compose
corrections.corrected: Add restart: unless-stopped to all three services in docker-compose.yml

new_threats_detected.pattern: Claude OAuth credentials (accessToken, refreshToken) mounted in plaintext read-only file
new_threats_detected.correction: Use Docker secrets for production, add file-level encryption, implement token rotation policy, limit credential scope to required capabilities only

new_threats_detected.pattern: Guardian script retry logic tries doctor --fix 3 times then git rollback without rate limiting or alert escalation
new_threats_detected.correction: Add exponential backoff (30s, 60s, 120s) instead of immediate retries. Add alerting on repeated failures. Prevent retry storms.

new_threats_detected.pattern: Gateway healthcheck endpoint /healthz exposed internally with no authentication
new_threats_detected.correction: Healthcheck endpoints are typically unauthenticated by design, but log access. Consider IP-based restrictions within docker network if feasible.

new_threats_detected.pattern: CLI container network_mode service:openclaw-gateway with cap_drop NET_RAW NET_ADMIN - incomplete capability restrictions
new_threats_detected.correction: Verify minimal capabilities. Consider cap_drop: ALL followed by cap_add for only required capabilities.

new_threats_detected.pattern: Guardian daily git backup of workspace - no retention policy or encryption specified
new_threats_detected.correction: Define retention (keep last N backups), add encryption for persistent backups, document backup verification testing.

new_threats_detected.pattern: claude-credentials.json file in host working directory - unencrypted OAuth tokens on Windows disk
new_threats_detected.correction: Never store OAuth credentials in plaintext on disk. Use Docker secrets, environment variables with .gitignore, encrypted vault, or Windows Credential Manager.

new_threats_detected.pattern: Docker-compose references openclaw-claude:local image without explicit version pinning or digest
new_threats_detected.correction: Pin image with digest or semantic version tag. Document build process. Never use local for production.

new_strategies_detected.pattern: Read-only volume mount (:ro flag) for credential files - prevents container from modifying mounted credentials
new_strategies_detected.explanation: Good defense-in-depth but not foolproof - prevents accidental modification but does not prevent theft. Combine with network policies.

new_strategies_detected.pattern: Loopback-only binding (127.0.0.1) for sensitive gateway service - blocks external network access at transport layer
new_strategies_detected.explanation: Excellent security pattern. Prevents accidental exposure. Still accessible to containers via service name. Complements application-layer auth.

new_strategies_detected.pattern: HTTP healthcheck endpoint instead of process detection - accounts for container isolation
new_strategies_detected.explanation: Proper Docker-aware health check. More reliable than pgrep. Consider timeout and retry settings on curl.

new_strategies_detected.pattern: Multi-tier failure recovery in Guardian: retry, doctor, rollback, cooldown - cascading error handling
new_strategies_detected.example: On failure: tries doctor --fix 3x, then git rollback, then cooldown
new_strategies_detected.explanation: Good intent but needs backoff strategy. Current implementation risks retry storms. Add exponential backoff and alert threshold.

new_strategies_detected.pattern: Claude OAuth max subscription tier in credentials - leveraging subscription level for access control
new_strategies_detected.explanation: Can be used for capability-based access control, but relies on OpenClaw not spoofing. Verify server-side.

new_strategies_detected.pattern: git safe.directory config in container to handle volume mount ownership mismatch - operational fix for POSIX/Windows permission bridge
new_strategies_detected.explanation: Practical workaround for Windows-Linux interop issue. Weaker than fixing ownership but maintains security gate.

summary: Session demonstrates strong security fundamentals: loopback binding, read-only credentials, proper USER directive, HTTP healthchecks, capability restrictions. Critical gaps: plaintext OAuth tokens on Windows disk (use vault/secrets), missing error handling in guardian.sh (add set -euo pipefail), credential theft risk (no scope/rotation). Key patterns: read-only mounts plus loopback binding are effective defense-in-depth. Recommend: (1) Move credentials to Docker secrets, (2) Add set -euo pipefail and exponential backoff to guardian, (3) Pin image versions, (4) Add restart: unless-stopped, (5) Document credential rotation SOP.
